sourceMovie.title: The Lobster
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: The Lobster (2015) stuck because it commits to its comedy premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Yorgos Lanthimos's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The comedy and drama blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Swiss Army Man
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Lobster clicked for you, Swiss Army Man (2016) is a strong next stop: both trade in comedy and drama, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after The Lobster?
faq.answer: Try Swiss Army Man, Seeking a Friend for the End of the World, Just Before I Go—each shares DNA with The Lobster while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is The Lobster?
faq.answer: The Lobster (2015) is best described as Comedy, Drama, Romance. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is The Lobster worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.0/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this comedy lane.
